message:
runtime/epoch_gc: fix stale underscore-prefixed param names in tracing::debug! macros

Both `src/runtime/epoch_gc.rs` (DeferredCleanupQueue) and
`src/runtime/epoch_gc_integration.rs` (the ObligationTable, IO-driver
waker, region state, timer, and channel integration wrappers) were
calling `tracing::debug!(obligation_id = _id, ...)` with
underscore-prefixed names such as `_id`, `_waker_id`, `_region_id`,
`_timer_id`, `_channel_id`, `_metadata`, `_data`, and `_task_id`.

Those underscore-prefixed names don't match the actual function
parameters any more — the signatures are all written without the
underscore prefix (`fn cleanup_obligation(&self, id: u64)`,
`fn cleanup_waker(&self, waker_id: u64, source: &str)`, etc.). The
code only compiles at all because `tracing-integration` is an
optional feature and most CI builds don't exercise these macros, so
the rustc "use of undeclared identifier" error stays behind the cfg.

Fix: drop the underscore prefix everywhere these identifiers appear
inside `tracing::debug!` / `tracing::trace!` in both files. Affected
sites:

crates/asupersync/src/runtime/epoch_gc.rs:
* `DeferredCleanupQueue::cleanup_obligation`: `_id` → `id`.
* `DeferredCleanupQueue::cleanup_waker`: `_waker_id` → `waker_id`.
* `DeferredCleanupQueue::cleanup_region`: `_region_id.as_u64()` →
  `region_id.as_u64()`, and in the inner per-task trace, both
  `_task_id.as_u64()` → `task_id.as_u64()` and
  `_region_id.as_u64()` → `region_id.as_u64()`.
* `DeferredCleanupQueue::cleanup_timer`: `_timer_id` → `timer_id`.
* `DeferredCleanupQueue::cleanup_channel`: `_channel_id` →
  `channel_id`.

crates/asupersync/src/runtime/epoch_gc_integration.rs:
* `ObligationTableEpochGC::cleanup_direct` (or sibling): `_obligation_id`
  → `obligation_id`, `_metadata.len()` → `metadata.len()`.
* `IODriverWakerEpochGC::cleanup_direct`: `_waker_id` → `waker_id`.
* `RegionStateEpochGC::cleanup_direct`: `_region_id.as_u64()` →
  `region_id.as_u64()`.
* `TimerEpochGC::cleanup_direct`: `_timer_id` → `timer_id`.
* `ChannelEpochGC::cleanup_direct`: `_channel_id` → `channel_id`,
  `_data.len()` → `data.len()`.

No behavior change — the tracing macros now reference parameters
that actually exist under `cfg(feature = "tracing-integration")`
builds, which is what they were always meant to do.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
